title: Architectural Approach to Level Design
creator: Christopher W. Totten
description: A second-edition guide that brings architectural history, spatial experience, circulation, landmarks, emotion, tutorials, and procedural methods into level-design practice.
useful: It treats a level as an inhabited space with perceptual and emotional structure, not merely a route between encounters.
audience: Level designers and environment artists who want a deeper spatial vocabulary.
learning: Apply circulation, landmarks, and spatial rhythm
Use architecture to shape emotion and navigation
Design tutorial spaces around player perception
